title: Clarify docker.io default in push with docker-daemon
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Touches up docker-deamon verbiage per @tliron.  Thank you for the suggested verbiage and apologies for taking so long to get this done!

Fixes: #987

comments.body: 👍 Thanks for taking care of this!

LGTM but I have some follow up questions.

- Do we need the same change for libpod?
- Does a similar behavior apply to other transports? If so, we could create a dedicate manpage for those transports (maybe in c/image?).

EDIT: I mean a manpage to describe all transports.

The `docker.io/library` normalization is common, but does not typically happen for `Buildah`/`Podman`; and `docker-daemon:` is special in that it has a particularly aggressive version of it, where it does not only _interpret_ short names with this semantics, but actively normalizes the name to explicitly include the hostname when contacting the daemon.

Yes, c/image should be maintaining a documentation of all its transports, and I can’t see it in there.  IIRC historically it lived in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/master/docs/skopeo.1.md , and yes it should be split into a separate man page referenced from the others.

:pushpin: Commit f8be42c has been approved by `rhatdan`

:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
